root_text.sn48.125-136:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gaṅgā nadī pācīnaninnā pācīnapoṇā pācīnapabbhārā;
root_text.sn48.125-136:1.2: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcindriyāni bhāvento pañcindriyāni bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro.
root_text.sn48.125-136:1.3: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcindriyāni bhāvento pañcindriyāni bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro?
root_text.sn48.125-136:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddhindriyaṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ …pe… paññindriyaṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ.
root_text.sn48.125-136:1.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcindriyāni bhāvento pañcindriyāni bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro”ti.

translation_text.sn48.125-136:0.2: 13. Another Chapter of Abbreviated Texts on the Ganges
translation_text.sn48.125-136:1.1: “Mendicants, the Ganges river slants, slopes, and inclines to the east.
translation_text.sn48.125-136:1.2: In the same way, a mendicant developing and cultivating the five faculties slants, slopes, and inclines to extinguishment.
translation_text.sn48.125-136:1.4: It’s when a mendicant develops the faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom, which culminate in the removal of greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.sn48.125-136:1.5: That’s how a mendicant developing and cultivating the five faculties slants, slopes, and inclines to extinguishment.”
translation_text.sn48.125-136:2.1: Six on slanting to the east,
translation_text.sn48.125-136:2.2: and six on slanting to the ocean;
translation_text.sn48.125-136:2.4: and that’s how this chapter is recited.
